concepts.title: Occurence of metals
concepts.conceptBody:
Occurrence of Metals

Element which have low chemical reactivity generally occur native or free or metallic state. e.g. $Au,\,Pt,\,$noble gas etc . Element which are chemically reactive, generally occur in the combined state . e.g. halogens, chalcogens etc. The natural materials in which the metals occur in the earth are called minerals . The mineral from which the metal is conveniently and economically extracted is called an ore . All the ores are minerals but all minerals cannot be ores.

concepts.title: Native ores
concepts.conceptBody: Ores Cheat Sheet Ores may be divided into four groups: 1. Metallic core (siderophile) of the earth crust contains (Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Ru, Rb, Pd, Ag, Re, Os, Ir, Pt, Au). Entire composition of metals in earth crust may be given as, $\mathrm{Al}(8.3\%) ; \mathrm{Ca}(3.6\%) ; \mathrm{Na}(2.8\%) ; \mathrm{K}(2.6\%) ; \mathrm{Mg}(2.1\%) ; \mathrm{Ti}(0.4\%) ; \mathrm{Mn}(0.1\%) ; \mathrm{Fe}(5.1\%)$ other metals $(0.1\%)$. Found. These are termed nuggets. Iron is found in free state as meteorites which also have 20 to $30\%$ nickel. 2. Sulphurised and arsenical ores: These ores consist of sulphides and arsenides in simple and complex forms of metals. Important ores of this group are: \[ \begin{array}{|c|l|c|} \hline \text{Metal} & \text{Name of the ore} & \text{Composition} \\ \hline \mathrm{Pb} & \text{Galena} & \mathrm{PbS} \\ \hline \mathrm{Zn} & \text{Zinc blende} & \mathrm{ZnS} \\ \hline \mathrm{Hg} & \text{Cinnabar} & \mathrm{HgS} \\ \hline \mathrm{Ag} & \text{Argentite} & \mathrm{Ag}_2 \mathrm{S} \\ \hline \mathrm{Fe} & \text{Iron pyrites} & \mathrm{FeS}_2 \\ \hline \mathrm{Ni} & \text{Kupfer nickel} & \mathrm{NiAs}_2 \\ \hline \mathrm{Cu} & \begin{array}{l} \text{Copper pyrites} \\ \text{or Copper glance} \end{array} & \begin{array}{l} \mathrm{CuFeS}_2 \\ \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{S} \end{array} \\ \hline \end{array} \] 3. Oxidised ores: In these ores, metals are present as their oxides or oxysalts such as carbonates, nitrates, sulphates, phosphates, silicates, etc. Important ores of this group are listed below. Oxides: \[ \begin{array}{|l|l|} \hline \text{Haematite} & \mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \\ \hline \text{Magnetite} & \mathrm{Fe}_3 \mathrm{O}_4 \\ \hline \text{Limonite} & \mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \cdot 3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \text{Bauxite} & \mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \text{Corundum} & \mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \\ \hline \text{Diaspore} & \mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \cdot \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \text{Chromite} & \mathrm{FeO} \cdot \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \\ \hline \text{Chromeochre} & \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \\ \hline \text{Tinstone (Cassiterite)} & \mathrm{SnO}_2 \\ \hline \text{Chrysoberyl} & \mathrm{BeO} \cdot \mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \\ \hline \text{Cuprite (Ruby copper)} & \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \text{Pyrolusite} & \mathrm{MnO}_2 \\ \hline \text{Zincite} & \mathrm{ZnO} \\ \hline \text{Rutile} & \mathrm{TiO}_2 \\ \hline \text{Ilmenite} & \mathrm{FeO} \cdot \mathrm{TiO}_2 \\ \hline \end{array} \] Carbonates: \[ \begin{array}{|l|l|} \hline \text{Magnesite} & \mathrm{MgCO}_3 \\ \hline \text{Limestone} & \mathrm{CaCO}_3 \\ \hline \text{Dolomite} & \mathrm{CaCO}_3 \cdot \mathrm{MgCO}_3 \\ \hline \text{Calamine} & \mathrm{ZnCO}_3 \\ \hline \text{Malachite} & \mathrm{CuCO}_3 \cdot \mathrm{Cu(OH)}_2 \\ \hline \text{Azurite} & \mathrm{Cu(OH)}_2 \cdot 2 \mathrm{CuCO}_3 \\ \hline \text{Cerussite} & \mathrm{PbCO}_3 \\ \hline \text{Siderite} & \mathrm{FeCO}_3 \\ \hline \end{array} \] Nitrates: \[ \begin{array}{|l|l|} \hline \text{Chile saltpetre} & \mathrm{NaNO}_3 \\ \hline \text{Saltpetre} & \mathrm{KNO}_3 \\ \hline \end{array} \] Sulphates: \[ \begin{array}{|l|l|} \hline \text{Epsom salt} & \mathrm{MgSO}_4 \cdot 7 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \text{Barytes} & \mathrm{BaSO}_4 \\ \hline \text{Gypsum} & \mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \text{Glauber's salt} & \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \cdot 10 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \text{Anglesite} & \mathrm{PbSO}_4 \\ \hline \text{Schonite} & \mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \cdot \mathrm{MgSO}_4 \cdot 6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \text{Polyhalite} & \mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \cdot \mathrm{MgSO}_4 \cdot \mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \end{array} \] Phosphates and Silicates: \[ \begin{array}{|l|l|} \hline \text{Lepidolite (an ore of lithium)} & (\mathrm{Li}, \mathrm{Na}, \mathrm{K})_2 \mathrm{Al}_2 \left(\mathrm{SiO}_3\right)_3 (\mathrm{F}, \mathrm{OH})_2 \\ \hline \text{Petalite (an ore of lithium)} & \mathrm{LiAl}\left(\mathrm{Si}_2 \mathrm{O}_5\right)_2 \\ \hline \text{Triphylite (an ore of lithium)} & (\mathrm{Li}, \mathrm{Na})_3 \mathrm{PO}_4, (\mathrm{Fe}, \mathrm{Mn})_3 \left(\mathrm{PO}_4\right)_2 \\ \hline \text{Beryl (an ore of berylium)} & 3 \mathrm{BeO} \cdot \mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \cdot 6 \mathrm{SiO}_2 \\ \hline \text{Willemite} & \mathrm{Zn}_2 \mathrm{SiO}_4 \\ \hline \text{China clay} & \mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \cdot 2 \mathrm{SiO}_2 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \text{Chlor-apatite} & 3 \mathrm{Ca}_3 \left(\mathrm{PO}_4\right)_2 \cdot \mathrm{CaCl}_2 \\ \hline \text{Mica} & \mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{O} \cdot 3 \mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \cdot 6 \mathrm{SiO}_2 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \text{Fluor-apatite} & 3 \mathrm{Ca}_3 \left(\mathrm{PO}_4\right)_2 \cdot \mathrm{CaF}_2 \\ \hline \text{Felspar} & \mathrm{KAlSi}_3 \mathrm{O}_8 \\ \hline \text{Talc} & \mathrm{Mg}_2 \left(\mathrm{Si}_2 \mathrm{O}_5\right) \cdot \mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \\ \hline \text{Asbestos} & \mathrm{CaMg}_3 \left(\mathrm{SiO}_3\right) \\ \hline \end{array} \]
